simulator_config.description: Pace-separation simulator defaults selected from diagnostic replay calibration; still not formal-ready without holdout validation.

provenance.geometry_proxy_note: Corner speed classes and straight counts are derived from stored corner angle/progress fields; not official speed or DRS/deployment-zone data.

route_notes: Adds to qualifying score before sampled grid order.

safety_car_probability.method: track-type prior derived from sourced circuit profile when available
track-type prior derived from sourced circuit profile when available

provenance.geometry_proxy_note: Corner speed classes and straight counts are derived from stored corner angle/progress fields; not official speed or DRS/deployment-zone data.

evidence.evidence_text: Formula1.com reported that George Russell beat Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton to Austrian GP pole after a late Max Verstappen crash.
evidence.reasoning: Pole at the Red Bull Ring is a positive race-week qualifying signal, while the late-session context keeps uncertainty above a fully clean performance read.

reasons: Historical archive proof supports cutoff availability.
Only one source currently supports this normalized claim direction.

evidence_impact.interpretation: small diagnostic sensitivity for qualifying_pace; computed by comparing the full prediction with a same-seed run that removes this claim only.
